articles.title: Bitcoin climbs as risk sentiment improves, altcoins hit by exploit concerns
articles.description: Bitcoin advanced while altcoins continued to struggle after the weekend's DeFi exploit, with markets eyeing Middle East tensions and shifting risk sentiment.

articles.title: Crypto scammers offer ‘safe passage’ through Hormuz. At least one ship may have been conned.
articles.description: Marisks, a maritime risk company, alerted ships stranded by the Hormuz blockade to scammers posing as Iranian authorities asking for bitcoin or USDT.

articles.title: Bank of Korea’s new governor signals CBDC and bank token push, skips stablecoins in key address
articles.description: The BOK will increase scrutiny of crypto markets and non-bank finance, and will also modernize currency markets for 24-hour foreign exchange trading, he said.

articles.title: Blue Energy raises $380M to build grid-scale nuclear reactors in shipyards
articles.description: By building reactors in shipyards, Blue Energy says it will tame nuclear power's cost problems, giving it access to cheaper financing.

articles.title: Scammers demand crypto from stranded ships in Strait of Hormuz: Report
articles.description: Shipping companies stranded near the Strait of Hormuz are reportedly being targeted by scammers posing as Iranian authorities, demanding Bitcoin or USDt for “safe passage.”

articles.title: Strategy overtakes BlackRock IBIT in bitcoin holdings after bear market buying

articles.title: Bitcoin is less volatile than South Korea's stock market right now
articles.description: Bitcoin's comparative stability during geopolitical turmoil has reinforced its appeal as a hedge.

articles.title: Philippines SEC warns on dYdX, six other unauthorized crypto platforms
articles.description: Promoters of flagged platforms may face fines of up to 5 million Philippine pesos ($89,000) or up to 21 years in prison under Philippine securities law.

articles.title: KelpDAO hackers are laundering millions in stolen crypto, data show
articles.description: KelpDAO hackers are moving $290M in stolen crypto across blockchains, using privacy tools to mask the trail as DeFi contagion fears move through the sector.

articles.title: Bank of Korea governor backs CBDCs, deposit tokens in first address
